notes:
Hoàn hảo means perfect — no mistakes, nothing missing, nothing to improve.  

It’s an adjective, used to describe things, results, people, plans, performances, etc. You can put it after a noun with là, or directly before a noun as a modifier.  

It often appears with words like rất, gần như, or không thể to soften or strengthen the idea, since true “perfection” is rare.  

Close synonym: tuyệt vời (amazing), but hoàn hảo focuses more on “no flaws.” Opposite: không hoàn hảo, có thiếu sót.
exampleSentencesTranslated: He always tries to do everything in the most perfect way possible.
This painting is really perfect, I can't find any mistakes at all.
I think the party yesterday was organized in a perfect and fun way.
notes_md:
**Hoàn hảo** means *perfect* — no mistakes, nothing missing, nothing to improve.  

It’s an adjective, used to describe things, results, people, plans, performances, etc. You can put it after a noun with **là**, or directly before a noun as a modifier.  

It often appears with words like *rất*, *gần như*, or *không thể* to soften or strengthen the idea, since true “perfection” is rare.  

Close synonym: **tuyệt vời** (amazing), but *hoàn hảo* focuses more on “no flaws.” Opposite: **không hoàn hảo**, **có thiếu sót**.

content.sentence: Mọi thứ đều trở nên ___ trong cuộc sống.

example.vi: Bức tranh này thật hoàn hảo.

definitions.es: Libre de cualquier defecto o imperfección; ideal.
definitions.vi: Không có bất kỳ khuyết điểm nào; lý tưởng.
definitions.en: Free from any flaws or defects; ideal.

exampleSentences: Anh ấy luôn cố gắng làm mọi việc một cách hoàn hảo nhất có thể.
Bức tranh này thật sự hoàn hảo, em không tìm thấy lỗi nào cả.
Chị nghĩ bữa tiệc hôm qua tổ chức rất hoàn hảo và vui vẻ.
